steps.title: Identify what the claim requires
steps.content: The passage states that Henry does not always provide comfort to Jimmie. The correct quotation must show Henry refusing or withholding comfort — not giving it, and not doing something unrelated. Look for language of rejection or criticism directed at Jimmie.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — "For one thing, Jimmie was invariably overcome with reverence when he was told how beautifully a harness was polished or a horse groomed.": Describes Jimmie's admiration for Henry's work, not Henry's response to Jimmie's shame. Does not address comfort. ✗
  • Choice B — "Henry... could usually provide a sin to place himself on a footing with the disgraced one. Perhaps he would remember that he had forgotten to put the hitching-strap in the back of the buggy on some recent occasion, and had been reprimanded by [Jimmie's father].": Shows Henry typically offering solidarity (a form of comfort) — the opposite of what the claim requires. ✗
  • Choice C — "Henry would sometimes choose to absolutely repudiate [Jimmie's behavior], and when Jimmie appeared in his shame would bully him most virtuously, preaching with assurance the precepts of [Jimmie's father's] creed, and pointing out to Jimmie all of his abominations.": 'Absolutely repudiate' and 'bully' show Henry withholding comfort and doing the opposite — scolding Jimmie instead. Directly illustrates the claim. ✓
  • Choice D — "Whenever Henry dwelt for a time in sackcloth [a symbol of guilt over wrongdoing], Jimmie did not patronize him at all.": Describes Jimmie's behavior toward Henry when Henry feels guilty, not Henry's response to Jimmie's shame. ✗
C is correct.
